screens.name: Songs library with inline harmonic notes
screens.purpose: Let the selected chart keep its normal lead-sheet scanability while exposing short theory notes directly on relevant bars.
screens.primary_action: Open a chart and read the harmonic annotations inline
states: selected:viewer open with inline measure analysis

ux_rules: Keep harmonic annotations visually subordinate to chord symbols.
Attach notes to the same measure cell as the target chord so interpretation remains local and fast to scan.
Do not add a separate sidebar or inspector for this first pass.

screen_contexts.layout_intent: Retain the existing two-pane reading surface and place short theory notes inside the measure cells without introducing avoidable overflow.
interaction_risks: Notes can visually overpower the chord labels.
Long note text can make representative rows too tall on narrow screens.
required_states: selected:viewer open with inline measure analysis
notes: Representative layout rows must still fit the reading pane width after the added notes appear.

constraints: Keep the change additive and backward-compatible with songs that do not have analysis notes.
Use concise Korean-language theory text for Goodbye.
Preserve the current editorial tone and avoid dashboard-like explanatory chrome.
open_decisions: Other songs can gain analysis notes later if the pattern proves useful.

critical_judgments: Chord symbols remain the visual priority.
The inline notes read like annotations attached to specific bars.
Representative rows keep pane-width fit on desktop and mobile.

visual_constraints: The note treatment should feel like an editorial annotation, not a callout card.
Avoid redundant local labels inside each measure.
